original_abstract:
This paper explores the development of social competence among high school students within the framework of non-formal education. The study involved a research experiment with 647 students across seven secondary schools in the Ternopil and Ivano-Frankivsk regions of Ukraine. During the pre-experimental phase, diagnostic methods were aligned with the core structural components of social competence, establishing clear criteria, indicators, and proficiency levels. Initial findings revealed a significant deficit in the social competence of prospective graduates.

To address this, specific socio-pedagogical conditions for non-formal education were implemented from September 2017 to June 2019. These interventions focused on fostering student agency, providing dedicated socio-pedagogical support and monitoring, modernizing educational content and methods, and creating spaces for student-led initiatives. The diverse forms of non-formal education introduced led to a measurable positive shift in the social competence of the experimental groups. Post-experimental results indicated a 15% increase in students achieving a high level of social competence and an 11% reduction in those at the low level. These findings validate the research hypothesis, demonstrating that shifting educational focus toward non-formal models significantly enhances the development of social competence in high school students.
humanized_abstract:
The article deals with the development of social competence of high school students in non-formal education settings. The research experiment carried out in seven secondary schools of Ternopil and Ivano-Frankivsk regions of Ukraine included 647 high school students. In the pre-experimental stage the diagnostic tools were adjusted to the main structural components of social competence and the criteria, indicators and levels of students’ proficiency were defined. It was established that there was a considerable shortage of social competence in students of secondary schools who are planned to become graduates in the near future.

In order to improve the situation concerning the socio-pedagogical conditions for non-formal education, the project implemented specific measures during the realization of the project from September 2017 to June 2019. The main target of these measures was to increase the student autonomy, provide socio-pedagogical support and control, modernize the educational material and methods and increase student’s initiative. The non-formal education forms implemented for various target groups contributed to the improvement of students’ social competence in the experimental groups. The data of the social competence of students after the implementation of the project showed an increase of 15% of students whose social competence was rated as high and at the same time a decrease of 11% of students whose social competence was rated as low. This shows that the research hypothesis of the project was confirmed and thus non-formal education forms implemented at the high school level have a positive influence on the development of social competence of high school students.
